material_world.name: Airth
material_world.description: Primary mortal realm, subject to natural laws, site of the Silence

faery.name: The Otherworld
faery.notes: Lizard-men and fey have mutual aversion

fire_sun.name: The Sun / Fire Plane
fire_sun.description: One and the same, vast beyond comprehension, 12000 times the surface area of the earth
fire_sun.threat: Source of fire elemental conspiracy to ignite moon (END-OF-WORLD THREAT)
fire_sun.scope: Only fire elementals relevant to current plot, other factions OUT OF SCOPE

corpse_planes.description: Dead gods' home planes, gods destroyed utterly and completely
corpse_planes.contents: Remnant divine power, ancient secrets, ultimate relics

moon.name: The Moon
moon.threat: Target of fire elemental ignition plot - if ignited: near-extinction event
moon.connection_to_gloaming: EXTENT UNDECIDED - serves as refuge, possible worship object, ancient memory repository

old_gods.temple_undead_status: Active, awaiting return that will never come
old_gods.temple_undead_knowledge: Either don't know gods are dead, refuse to accept it, or suspect but don't speak (taboo)

new_gods.origin: Irrelevant to campaign, no memory of creation

malac.name: Malac
malac.factions_note: UNDECIDED - bad faction sympathetic to fire elementals, unwittingly helping conspiracy

paramandir.name: Paramandir
paramandir.status: Mythic neutral paladin who bound Urzael to the moon

fey.sun_metal_crafting: Covenant-Bound can fashion items/weapons from sun-metal

necromancy.type: Blend of old divine and arcane methods
necromancy.locations: 31 Temples of Dead Gods (including one north of Tagmira)

velgroth_lucernia.name: Velgroth-Lucernia
velgroth_lucernia.government: High Kingship (multiple petty kingdoms under nominal High King)
velgroth_lucernia.recent_history: Past 50 years: No inter-kingdom warfare (unprecedented peace)

velgroth.status: Recovering seafaring tradition, exploring wild coasts
velgroth.exploration: Seeking other human settlements with no success

lucernia.history: Was Sparta-like but ardor has cooled, collectivist culture fading

luric_marches.name: The Luric Marches
luric_marches.location: Between civilized lands and wilderness, east of Lucernia, west of Imazuran

example_settlements: Caelrun (most residents unaware of lizard-man control)

irkali.name: The Five Irkali (Marhaz)
irkali.description: Sites where New Gods first manifested

elves.presence: Present almost everywhere but in small numbers
elves.restriction: ALL elves barred from sacred sites

forsworn.status: Severed from Faery and returned to mortal life

covenant_bound.status: Embraced fey inheritance, serve The Pact-Render

gray_kin.relationship_to_pact_render: Respect their goals but not their methods

lizard_men.overview: Apex predators and dominant species of the swamp. Rational, organized, and possessed of a sophisticated material culture. Human settlements exist in swamp territory by lizard-man sufferance, though most human residents are unaware of this. Lizard-men are not a faction — they are a population, with ideological tendencies operating across the whole.

ecology.role: Apex predator — hunt and eat other predators as well as lesser life forms
diet: Eggs of all kinds (staple and prized)
ecology.preferred_terrain: Swamp and wetland. Capable of inhabiting bodies of water. Forests and plains are unpleasant; prolonged exposure to dry terrain is debilitating. Expansion into non-swamp biomes is currently impractical.
ecology.population: High. The swamp, in places, is intensively managed to support lizard-man population density.

agriculture.character: Alien in appearance to human eyes — what looks like wilderness is often managed land. Channels are not random. Log structures are not debris.

systems.name: Egg nurseries
systems.description: Controlled mud-heated incubation beds for reptile, amphibian, and bird eggs. Managed like orchards — seeded, tended, harvested.

systems.name: Insect colonies
systems.description: Farmed in hollow logs and constructed mounds. Termites, beetles, and grubs at scale.

systems.name: Fish ponds
systems.description: Channeled and weirèd sections of swamp managed for sustained yield.

systems.name: Fungus cultivation
systems.description: Rotting wood structures seeded and harvested. Multiple species maintained.

systems.name: Managed hunting grounds
systems.description: Sections of swamp where prey populations are deliberately maintained and culled rather than exhausted.

froggish_folk.role: Labor force. Live among lizard-men in a state of subjugation but not outright misery.
froggish_folk.status: Too simple to conceive of an alternative. Accept their condition without resentment.
froggish_folk.material_conditions: Relatively comfortable by their own standards. Lizard-man shamans provide magical services the froggish folk cannot generate themselves — a genuine exchange, if unequal.
labor_types: Aquatic labor — harvesting weirs, clearing channels, retrieving submerged eggs
Messengers — fast, small, unnoticed in water and reed
Lookouts — station in shallows and reeds, can submerge for extended periods
Entertainers — communal chorus; lizard-men find it genuinely pleasurable
froggish_folk.free_froggish_folk: A population of froggish folk live outside lizard-man settlements. They have freedom but no shamanic umbrella.

faction_attitudes.clean_death: Kind and fair toward froggish folk
faction_attitudes.restrained_hunger: Cruel; occasionally eats them — rare but not unknown
faction_attitudes.long_work: Largely indifferent; does not engage much

religion.notes: All lizard-men worship the same pantheon regardless of ideological faction. The ideological split is ethical, not religious — the Restrained Hunger cannot be cast out as heretics. They worship the same gods and merely interpret the license those gods grant differently.
religion.shamanic_tradition: Respected by all factions. Lizard-man shamans can cast spells; this is a point of distinction from the froggish folk and a source of cultural pride.

leeandra.role: Cross-faction diplomat. Stands outside all three ideological tendencies.
leeandra.mission: Maintain an uneasy peace between the three factions, and between lizard-men collectively and humanity.
leeandra.status: Trusted and resented in equal measure by all factions. Her neutrality is both her authority and her vulnerability.
leeandra.significance: Load-bearing for the stability of the Luric Marches. Her removal would be catastrophic.

diplomacy.attitude_toward_diplomacy: All three factions support Leeandra and the broader diplomatic project. They are rational beings who understand that internal conflict weakens them collectively.

ideological_factions._note: These are tendencies, not organizations. They are ideological — not territorial, not hereditary. Brothers and sisters may hold different views. All factions share the same pantheon, respect the shamanic tradition, and support lizard-man diplomatic solidarity.

the_clean_death.name: The Clean Death
the_clean_death.ethos: Minimize suffering in the kill. Death is necessary; cruelty is not.
the_clean_death.worldview: Pluralist. See lizard-men as one people among many equals — alongside humans, elves, orcs, hobgoblins. Coexistence is not just strategic; it is correct.
the_clean_death.attitude_toward_expansion: Content within the swamp. Want stable, lasting relations with neighboring peoples.
the_clean_death.attitude_toward_humanity: Most open of the three factions. Genuine belief in mutual respect.
the_clean_death.coalition_role: Natural ally to Leeandra. The ideological anchor of any anti-Restrained Hunger coalition — but uncomfortable partnering with the Long Work, whose endgame contradicts Clean Death values.

the_restrained_hunger.name: The Restrained Hunger
the_restrained_hunger.ethos: The hunger for sentient humanoid flesh is real and present. They refrain — for now. They frame this restraint as discipline and virtue, not as shame.
the_restrained_hunger.worldview: Inward-focused. The threat they pose is visceral and personal, not political or territorial.
the_restrained_hunger.attitude_toward_expansion: Not expansionist. The danger they represent is not conquest but appetite.
the_restrained_hunger.attitude_toward_humanity: Suppress a genuine desire to eat them. The restraint is real but fragile.
the_restrained_hunger.attitude_toward_froggish_folk: Cruel. Occasionally eats them — rare, but it happens.
the_restrained_hunger.attitude_toward_goblins: Will eat them if opportunity arises. Goblins are low-status enough that there are no consequences.
the_restrained_hunger.coalition_role: The threat that forces the Clean Death and the Long Work into uneasy alliance despite their disagreements. Support lizard-man solidarity but are a powder keg within it.

the_long_work.name: The Long Work
the_long_work.ethos: Humane killers, like the Clean Death. But where the Clean Death accept the current world, the Long Work intend to change it — slowly, over centuries.
the_long_work.worldview: Expansionist. See territorial growth as an engineering problem: divert water, flood plains, convert biomes, make the Marches progressively less hospitable to human settlement. No urgency. No aggression. Just patient work.
the_long_work.attitude_toward_expansion: The defining characteristic. Their agricultural sophistication is the engine of a centuries-long territorial ambition. They are not at war with humanity — they are redesigning the landscape.
the_long_work.attitude_toward_humanity: Not hostile. Not peers either. Humanity will retreat as the swamp advances; this is simply how the world will go.
the_long_work.attitude_toward_froggish_folk: Largely indifferent. Neither kind nor cruel.
the_long_work.coalition_role: The necessary but uncomfortable partner in any alliance against the Restrained Hunger. The Clean Death must accept that the Long Work's endgame is the slow displacement of humanity — a genuine moral problem for the coalition, and a lever for the party once they understand it.

lizard_men.taboo: Teaching the lizard-man language to outsiders
lizard_men.human_settlements: Caelrun exists by lizard-man sufferance. Most residents are unaware of this. Other human settlements in swamp territory operate under similar conditions.

goblins.threat_level: Only dangerous in groups, individually nuisance

hungerbound.factions_note: UNDECIDED - even the good are terrible foes; examples NEED DEVELOPMENT

zimru_irkala.title: Hierophant
zimru_irkala.belief: Gods merely 'silent' not dead OR knows but maintains order

fire_elementals.origin: The Sun (which IS the Fire Plane)
fire_elementals.scope: Other fire plane factions OUT OF SCOPE

moon_creatures.stakes: Will die if moon is ignited

ardanir.name: The Ardanir
ardanir.tension: Won't kneel to mortal authority (High King, petty kings)

serdanos.name: The Serdanos (Rootbound)
serdanos.warning: Making Faery a faith caused post-Fall horrors

ilmenor.name: The Ilmenor
ilmenor.goal: Eventually reforge unified arcana via political influence

covenant_bound.name: The Covenant-Bound
covenant_bound.goal: Turn the earth into another Faery using humanoids as tools (think they can control them, but cannot control men). Seek revenge for crimes committed against fey by humans during The Deepening, and assurance it can never happen again.
covenant_bound.relationship_to_fire_elementals: Think they are using fire elementals' power to their advantage, unaware of plan to ignite moon
covenant_bound.relationship_to_sun_metal: NEEDS DEVELOPMENT - Why can they work it? What do they make?

gloaming_court.name: The Gloaming Court
gloaming_court.leader: Aislith Who Wove the First Garland
gloaming_court.goal: Collect memories of the lost human/faery connection
gloaming_court.potential_role: Oppose fire elementals if moon is power source?

periods.label: The Rising
periods.note: Last 50 years: No warfare between Velgroth-Lucernian kingdoms

the_deepening.description: Crimes committed against fey by humans - Covenant-Bound seek revenge for this period

emergence_of_irkali.description: New Gods appeared at sacred sites

establishment_of_high_kingship.description: TO BE DEVELOPED - when? how? why peace for 50 years?

properties.temperature: Warm to touch, never cools completely

sources: Ogre Magi priests craft with it
distribution.appears_in: Humanoid hands (armed by Covenant-Bound or fire cultists)

sun_metal.possible_purposes: NEEDS DECISION - ritual component? anti-magic? planar anchor? life-draining? other?
